data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to efficiently manage its backend.
data.technical_spec: Each service is independently deployable and handles a specific function (e.g., messaging, notifications).

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Messages are encrypted on the sender’s device and only decrypted on the recipient’s device.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Balances incoming requests across multiple servers to optimize resource utilization.
data.technical_spec: Employs algorithms like round-robin and least connections.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: Built to handle billions of active users and message transactions.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes cloud services with auto-scaling capabilities.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: System designed to respond to user actions and changes in real-time.
data.technical_spec: Implements event streaming and message queuing.
